behavior_summary: When the player examines (looks at) the SKELETON actor, the U8P_SKELETON::look process emits a single short bark reading "skeleton " and then suspends/ends.
There are no menu choices, flag reads/writes, external calls, or branching conditions; the behavior is purely presentational (a description/bark).
The process appears to be the actor's 'U8P_SKELETON::look' handler (process type 0x0208) and is triggered by the engine's examine/U8P_SKELETON::look action.
notable_control_flow: U8P_SKELETON::look executes, issues a Bark node with text "skeleton ", then reaches a suspend/terminate node — so control returns to the engine after the short description.
open_questions: Does the trailing space in the bark text "skeleton " indicate concatenation with another string elsewhere, or is it simply the full displayed text?
Does the engine treat the suspend as an end-of-process cleanup, or can this process be resumed by other engine events? (suspend meaning inferred from pattern)

main_function.name: U8P_SKELETON::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description when the player examines or 'looks at' the SKELETON actor by showing a single bark and then suspending the process.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the game engine when the player uses the U8P_SKELETON::look/examine action on the SKELETON actor (actor U8P_SKELETON::look handler / process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_SKELETON::look
function_map.role: Emit a short descriptive bark for the skeleton when examined; then suspend/end the U8P_SKELETON::look process.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the player examines (looks at) the SKELETON actor, the U8P_SKELETON::look process emits a single short bark reading "skeleton " and then suspends/ends.
